markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ decision to push Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, after two prior delays, has given traders a six-month window that makes events like a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire or Drake’s Iceman album release appear highly likely before launch. Recent pre-order leaks and Take-Two’s upcoming May earnings call could trigger another trailer or further schedule tweaks, while the game’s $1.5 billion development budget and historical Rockstar polish cycles continue to shape expectations. With the long runway, market-implied odds reflect broad consensus that major cultural milestones will land first, though any surprise delay or accelerated marketing push could quickly shift momentum for remaining outcomes.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s New York rape retrial has sharply boosted trader consensus around no additional prison time, as the deadlocked jury leaves that charge unresolved for a potential fourth trial. Weinstein, now 74 and in declining health, continues serving a 16-year California sentence from his 2022 conviction while appealing both that ruling and a separate upheld New York sexual-assault finding that could add up to 25 years. With ongoing appeals, consecutive-sentence questions, and his advanced age, market-implied odds heavily favor scenarios where he avoids extended further incarceration. Upcoming decisions on whether prosecutors retry the mistrial case or how appeals courts rule on his existing terms remain the key near-term catalysts that could shift these probabilities.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities around the Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes reflects both clubs’ strong regular-season standings, elite goaltending depth, and proven postseason experience heading into the 2025-26 playoffs. Traders see the Avalanche’s veteran core and the Hurricanes’ structured defensive play as balancing factors against a league defined by parity, keeping their respective chances nearly even. The Vegas Golden Knights sit further back after solid regular-season results but face tougher projected paths, while the Montreal Canadiens and Buffalo Sabres trail due to ongoing roster development and shorter playoff histories. This competitive distribution highlights how minor shifts in injury reports, series outcomes, or momentum can rapidly reshape the field.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Oklahoma City Thunder hold the strongest position among 2026 NBA title contenders due to their dominant playoff run, including sweeps of the Suns and Lakers that underscore elite depth, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's consistent scoring, and defensive versatility entering the Western Conference Finals against the Spurs. In the East, the New York Knicks' strong semifinal performance against Philadelphia creates a realistic path, while the Detroit Pistons and Cleveland Cavaliers remain longer shots amid their ongoing seven-game series. Trader consensus reflects these recent results and historical patterns for young, healthy cores in late-spring matchups.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2026 FIFA World Cup just weeks away, trader sentiment remains tightly bunched among top contenders because several European and South American sides boast comparable squad depth, recent international success, and proven tournament pedigree. Spain's status as European champions and extended unbeaten run underpin their strong positioning, while France's blend of experience and attacking talent from prior World Cup campaigns keeps them neck-and-neck. Defending champions Argentina benefit from core continuity, and England, Brazil, and Germany maintain realistic paths through strong qualifying form and favorable early groupings. This balance of high-quality depth across multiple nations, combined with the expanded 48-team format's inherent variability, sustains competitive implied probabilities without any single side pulling decisively ahead.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2026 FIFA World Cup just weeks away, trader sentiment remains tightly bunched among top contenders because several European and South American sides boast comparable squad depth, recent international success, and proven tournament pedigree. Spain's status as European champions and extended unbeaten run underpin their strong positioning, while France's blend of experience and attacking talent from prior World Cup campaigns keeps them neck-and-neck. Defending champions Argentina benefit from core continuity, and England, Brazil, and Germany maintain realistic paths through strong qualifying form and favorable early groupings. This balance of high-quality depth across multiple nations, combined with the expanded 48-team format's inherent variability, sustains competitive implied probabilities without any single side pulling decisively ahead.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's continued dominance as General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party underpins the 93 percent trader consensus against his removal before 2027. Recent military purges, including the January 2026 investigations of senior Central Military Commission figures, have further centralized authority and eliminated potential rivals through ongoing anti-corruption and loyalty enforcement measures. The March 2026 National People's Congress sessions proceeded without disruption, advancing the 15th Five-Year Plan and signaling institutional stability ahead of the 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027. Analysts note that Xi faces no visible factional challenges or procedural barriers to a fourth term, though health developments or unexpected elite shifts remain theoretical variables that could still influence outcomes.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential primary, traders assign Gavin Newsom the highest implied probability at 24.4 percent, reflecting his established national profile as California governor, extensive fundraising network, and experience navigating high-visibility policy debates. A fragmented field follows, with Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris each at 8.6 percent and other contenders like Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro clustered below 7 percent, underscoring the absence of a clear consensus after the prior cycle. Key differentiators include name recognition, state-level governing records, and appeal to distinct party coalitions, while consolidation could occur through early caucus and primary results, major endorsements, or shifts in polling averages across battleground states. The 2026 midterms may further test emerging contenders and influence voter turnout patterns that shape longer-term probabilities.
